title: Llama7B attention/KV compute floor gap
hypothesis: The merged measured NPU compute PPA remains well below the Llama7B 131k native-GQA KV8 HBM-bound compute floor; if true, deeper SRAM/NoC arbitration simulation should wait until a denser compute architecture is identified.

direct_comparison.primary_question: How far is the current measured NPU compute density from the first HBM-bound Llama7B 131k attention/KV compute floor?
include: Merged physical HBM compute-sensitivity result for Llama7B 131k native-GQA KV8
follow_on_broad_sweep: If the measured density reaches the floor, run deeper SRAM/NoC arbitration sensitivity
If the measured density is below the floor, define a denser clustered compute RTL target first

expected_benefit: Prevents spending effort on detailed NoC simulation while the design is still compute-bound by a large margin
Quantifies the required MAC/cycle/mm2 multiplier for the next compute architecture
Connects the abstract HBM-bound floor to measured compute PPA evidence
risks: The floor comes from an analytic physical-HBM planning model
Measured compute density is based on existing nm-style blocks and may not represent a purpose-built dense array
The result is a planning gate, not a final architecture decision

required_evaluations.objective: Quantify how far merged measured NPU compute PPA is from the Llama7B 131k HBM-bound compute floor before requesting deeper SRAM/NoC arbitration simulation.

expected_result.reason: The job should report the MAC/cycle and MAC/cycle/mm2 multiplier needed for measured compute blocks to reach the HBM-bound attention floor.

knowledge_refs: discussion:2026-06-03 compute floor must be physically plausible before detailed memory/NoC simulation
